description_long: This bungalow features a steeply pitched gable roof with dark shingles and a prominent front porch covered by a smaller gabled roof. The exterior walls are clad in light-colored brick with darker brick accents, giving the house a warm, inviting appearance. Large vertical windows dominate the front facade, allowing ample natural light into the main living areas, while smaller windows are distributed asymmetrically on the sides and rear. The entryway is centrally located beneath the porch, with a single door and a small set of steps leading up to it. The roof includes a chimney and a vent, and the overall structure is compact yet spacious, typical of suburban residential neighborhoods. The house sits on a flat, paved base, suggesting integration with urban sidewalks or driveways.
description: A light brick bungalow with a steep gable roof, prominent front porch, and large vertical windows on the front facade. The design is compact, with asymmetrical window placement and dark roof shingles.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, covered porch, and large vertical windows dominating the facade.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the steep roof pitch and two vertically aligned windows, with minimal architectural detailing.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a secondary entrance, two smaller windows, and the continuation of the gable roof.
description_view_3: Right side view with two vertically stacked windows and a clear view of the roof's slope and chimney.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical with large front vertical windows and scattered side windows
